answers: Improv (improvisation) is unscripted, spontaneous comedy or theater created in the moment. Performers make up scenes, characters, and dialogue on the spot, often based on audience suggestions. It's thrilling and unpredictable!

The golden rule of improv is 'Yes, And...' — accept what your scene partner says and build on it. Never deny or block their ideas. Other key principles include listening actively, making your partner look good, and committing fully to choices.

Famous improv groups include The Second City (Chicago), UCB (Upright Citizens Brigade), and Groundlings. Many famous comedians started in improv — Tina Fey, Amy Poehler, Steve Carell, and Ryan Stiles.

Learning improv improves communication skills, quick thinking, confidence, and teamwork. It's used in business training, therapy, and education too. Many cities have improv classes and workshops open to beginners.
